message:
fix(hardening): remove byte-indexed string slicing from display & parse paths

Every `&s[..N]` in RCH is a latent panic the moment the string is shorter
than `N` bytes or crosses a UTF-8 boundary at byte `N`. Several of these
sit directly on paths that accept hostile input — verification manifests
from workers, `.env` files from the user's project, worker telemetry
strings — so a crafted or merely malformed byte can crash the whole
hook/daemon.

Introduces a shared `rch_common::util::truncate_at_char_boundary(&str, max)`
helper that snaps back to the nearest UTF-8 boundary, and a second
`short_hash(&str)` helper scoped to artifact-hash display.

Migrations:

  rch-common/src/util.rs
    + truncate_at_char_boundary helper + four unit tests covering
      ASCII, multi-byte, mid-codepoint snap-back, and zero-length.

  rch-common/src/artifact_verify.rs
    - `&failure.expected_hash[..16]` / `&failure.actual_hash[..16]`
    + `short_hash(&failure.{expected,actual}_hash)` — char-indexed
      truncation that tolerates `{"hash":"ab"}` (short) or a hash
      containing a multi-byte character. Adds regression tests for
      both cases.

  rch-common/src/config/dotenv.rs
    Lone-quote values like `KEY="` previously entered the
    `value[1..value.len() - 1]` branch, computed `value[1..0]`, and
    panicked. Now requires `value.len() >= 2` before treating a value
    as quoted. Regression test: `LONE_DOUBLE="`, `LONE_SINGLE='`,
    `EMPTY=` must all parse without panicking.

  rch/src/commands/config.rs
    config_diff: current/default truncation now uses helper.

  rch/src/commands/queue.rs
    queue_status: build.command truncation (two call sites) uses helper.

  rch/src/status_display.rs
    render_circuit_details_to: worker.last_error truncation uses helper.
    render_builds_section_to: build.command truncation (two call sites,
    widths 50 and 40) uses helper.

No behavior change on ASCII-only strings shorter than the limit; failures
that used to panic now silently fall back to a non-empty, valid-UTF-8
truncated string.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
